models.generation_context: Fifth-generation T1 platform, introduced 2019; mid-cycle refresh 2022 with redesigned interior. 2026 brings updated pricing and continued availability of 2.7L turbo, 5.3L V8, 6.2L V8, and 3.0L Duramax diesel powertrains.
models.model_summary: GMC's full-size light-duty pickup, mechanically twin to the Chevrolet Silverado 1500 but with a more upscale interior trim and dedicated Denali and AT4X variants. Eight trims span work-truck to luxury, four engine choices (2.7L turbo I4, 5.3L V8, 6.2L V8, 3.0L turbo-diesel I6), and crew/double/regular cab body styles.

reliability.summary: GMC ranked 24th overall at 228 PP100 in the JD Power 2026 Vehicle Dependability Study (released Feb 2026), well below the 204 industry average — a notable decline from its 7th-place 2025 finish. Consumer Reports 2026 ranked GMC 23rd among brands with a brand score of 31/100; GMC was singled out among the lowest-scoring brands. Sierra 1500 predicted reliability is below average (2/5) based on 2023-2025 model-year data.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Sierra 1500 as a capable, well-built full-size truck with a luxurious Denali Ultimate trim that rivals premium SUVs, but note that interior tech, ride quality, and powertrain refinement still trail class leaders. Edmunds rates it 6.7/10, citing a varied engine lineup, strong towing (up to 13,200 lb), and a polished cabin offset by an aging platform awaiting the rumored 2027 refresh.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the truck's exterior styling, interior comfort, and ride quality on Denali trims; common complaints include perceived value at higher trim levels and occasional infotainment connectivity issues with Apple CarPlay and Android Auto.

trims.notes: Base work truck. Regular Cab Standard Bed 2WD starting MSRP. NHTSA assigned 5-star overall rating to the 2026 Sierra 1500 Crew Cab; IIHS did not award TSP for 2026 (Marginal small overlap, Acceptable side, no TSP designation).
Available with 2.7L Turbo, 5.3L V8, or 3.0L Duramax diesel (optional). Crew Cab configuration available at additional cost.

notable_other: Heated front seats and steering wheel standard

trims.notes: Elevation is a styling/equipment tier emphasizing blacked-out trim. 5.3L V8 standard with 10-speed automatic.

notable_other: Bose premium audio standard from SLT up

trims.notes: Mid-tier luxury trim. 5.3L V8 standard; 6.2L V8 and 3.0L Duramax diesel optional.
Off-road-focused 4WD trim. Crew Cab Short Bed standard.

notable_other: Front and rear electronic locking differentials

trims.notes: Top off-road trim. 6.2L V8 standard with electronic lockers; Duramax 3.0L diesel optional.
Luxury flagship trim. Super Cruise is optional. Premium leather and open-pore wood trim.

notable_other: Super Cruise standard with hands-free trailering
Full-grain leather with massaging 16-way power front seats

trims.notes: Flagship luxury trim with Super Cruise standard. 6.2L V8 standard; 3.0L Duramax diesel optional.

Base trim of the diesel powertrain line. MSRP approximates the diesel-engine $3,000 option premium on the Pro trim. Powertrain warranty for diesel extends to 5yr/100k. Most fuel-efficient Sierra 1500 with 25 mpg combined. Treated as a separate base trim per multi-powertrain rule.

models.notes: Eight trims researched plus a separate Pro Diesel base for the diesel powertrain line (per multi-powertrain rule). All trims share the GMC Pro Safety standard ADAS suite (AEB, lane keep, lane departure, automatic high beams); higher trims add blind spot, rear cross, ACC, and Super Cruise. NHTSA awarded 5-star overall to the 2026 Sierra 1500; IIHS did NOT award TSP/TSP+ for 2026 due to Marginal small overlap rating. Image entries marked needs_scraping for follow-up image extraction in Phase 4.

models.generation_context: Fifth-generation T1XX HD platform refreshed for 2024 with new exterior, interior overhaul (13.4-inch infotainment, 12.3-inch driver display), 6.6L Duramax diesel upgraded to 470 hp / 975 lb-ft. 2026 carries over with modest pricing updates.
models.model_summary: GMC's heavy-duty pickup lineup, available in 2500HD and 3500HD configurations. Standard 6.6L gas V8 (401 hp/464 lb-ft) or available 6.6L Duramax turbo-diesel V8 (470 hp/975 lb-ft), both paired with an Allison 10-speed automatic. 2500HD tows up to 22,500 lb; 3500HD up to 36,000 lb (gooseneck) and tops out at the Denali Ultimate flagship at $99,795.

reliability.summary: GMC ranked 24th overall at 228 PP100 in the JD Power 2026 Vehicle Dependability Study, well below the 204 industry average. HD-specific scores not separately published. Consumer Reports 2026 ranked GMC 23rd (31/100) — among the lowest-scoring brands. RepairPal gives the Sierra 2500HD a 3.0/5 reliability rating ranked #10 of 19 full-size trucks.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July). HD pickup segment also typically receives limited APEAL coverage.

professional_reviews.summary: The 2026 Sierra HD is praised for its enormous towing capability (up to 36,000 lb with 3500HD gooseneck) and the upgraded 470-hp/975 lb-ft Duramax diesel that arrived in 2024. Reviewers like the modernized interior with 13.4-inch infotainment and the Denali Ultimate's exclusive features, but note the gas 6.6L V8 still uses a 6-speed (not the Allison 10-speed which is diesel-only). Edmunds rates both 2500HD and 3500HD around 4/5 from owners.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise towing capability, comfortable ride, multi-camera views, and large touchscreen. Common complaints: occasionally uncomfortable seating, infotainment/connectivity glitches, and reports of factory build-quality issues.

trims.notes: Base 2500HD work truck. Heavy-duty pickups (Class 2b/3) are exempt from EPA fuel economy testing; no EPA MPG data published. NHTSA/IIHS do not test heavy-duty pickups; safety ratings null. Regular Cab Long Bed 2WD starting MSRP.
Step-up mid-volume 2500HD trim. Crew Cab optional. Duramax diesel optional.
Mid-luxury 2500HD. Duramax diesel widely optioned. Most Sierra HD owners pick this trim or above.
Off-road-focused 2500HD trim. Duramax diesel widely optioned.

notable_other: Front and rear electronic locking differentials

trims.notes: Top off-road HD trim. Duramax diesel standard with 5yr/100k powertrain warranty.
Luxury 2500HD trim. Super Cruise hands-free is an option.

notable_other: Super Cruise standard with hands-free trailering
Full-grain leather with massaging 16-way power front seats

trims.notes: Flagship 2500HD trim. Diesel standard, Super Cruise standard.

Base 3500HD work truck. Heavy-duty (Class 3) exempt from EPA testing. Regular Cab Long Bed 2WD SRW starting price; DRW (dual rear wheel) configurations cost more. Up to 36,000 lb gooseneck tow when properly equipped.
Step-up 3500HD trim. Crew Cab and Duramax diesel optional.
Mid-luxury 3500HD. Duramax diesel optional.
Off-road-focused 3500HD trim. Duramax diesel widely optioned. No AT4X variant offered for 3500HD.
Luxury 3500HD trim. Super Cruise hands-free is an option.

notable_other: Super Cruise standard with hands-free trailering
Full-grain leather with massaging 16-way power front seats

trims.notes: Flagship 3500HD trim. Diesel standard, Super Cruise standard. Up to 36,000 lb gooseneck towing capacity.

models.notes: Two distinct trim_family groups for the 2500HD and 3500HD body styles within the same Sierra HD model entry. Each (2500HD and 3500HD) has its own Pro base trim per multi-platform/sub-model rule. Heavy-duty trucks are exempt from EPA fuel economy testing under 40 CFR 600.114-08 (rated GVWR > 8500 lb), and NHTSA/IIHS do not crash-test heavy-duty trucks in this segment; fuel_economy MPG values and safety ratings null with notes. Standard GMC Pro Safety on every trim (AEB, lane keep, lane departure); higher trims add blind spot, rear cross, ACC, and Super Cruise. Image entries marked needs_scraping for follow-up image extraction in Phase 4.

models.generation_context: Third-generation, introduced for 2023 MY with a single-engine 2.7L TurboMax I4 strategy and Crew Cab only. AT4X returns as a standalone trim in 2026 after being a package in 2025.
models.model_summary: GMC's midsize pickup, mechanically shared with the Chevrolet Colorado. Single Crew Cab Short Box body style with one engine (2.7L TurboMax turbo I4, 310 hp / 430 lb-ft) and 8-speed automatic. Four trims (Elevation, AT4, Denali, AT4X) span on-road to serious off-road duty.

reliability.summary: GMC ranked 24th overall at 228 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (released Feb 2026), well below the 204 industry average. CR 2026 ranked GMC 23rd (31/100). Canyon shares Colorado mechanicals which have had electrical-system and infotainment recalls in 2023-2024 launch period; Consumer Reports predicts below-average reliability based on early-MY data.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Canyon's bold styling, strong 310-hp turbo-four, and serious off-road capability of the AT4X. Edmunds rates it 6.3/10, citing comfort, towing (up to 7,700 lb), and the unique single-engine strategy. Knocks include below-average reliability, occasional infotainment glitches, and pricing creeping toward full-size territory at higher trims.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the Canyon's smooth ride, strong power, and spacious interior. Complaints concentrate on perceived reliability — electronic glitches, battery issues — and value-for-money relative to similarly priced full-size trucks.

trims.notes: Base Elevation trim, 2WD Crew Cab Short Box. IIHS tested 2026 Canyon: Good small overlap front, Good side, but Poor vehicle-to-vehicle front crash prevention — no TSP. NHTSA 2026 rating not yet published. 4WD Elevation available at $44,295.

notable_other: AT4 off-road suspension and skid plates
8-way power heated/ventilated front seats with leather

trims.notes: Off-road-oriented trim with 4WD standard.

notable_other: Teak interior accents with laser-etched wood trim

trims.notes: Luxury Canyon trim. Crew Cab Short Box 4WD only.

notable_other: Front and rear electronic locking differentials

trims.notes: Top off-road Canyon trim, returned as a standalone trim for 2026 after being a package in 2025. AT4X AEV Edition adds beadlock-capable wheels, 35-inch tires, and underbody armor as a $10,100 option package (not a separate trim per spec rules).

models.notes: Four trims, all with the single 2.7L TurboMax I4 engine. AT4X AEV Edition is a $10,100 option package on top of AT4X, not a separate trim. IIHS rated the 2026 Canyon Crew Cab with mixed results (Good front/side crashworthiness but Poor front crash prevention vehicle-to-vehicle) — no TSP/TSP+ awarded. NHTSA had not published 2026 ratings as of research date; 2023 NHTSA crash data carries forward. Image entries marked needs_scraping for follow-up image extraction in Phase 4.

models.generation_context: First-generation Sierra EV on GM's Ultium platform; production began 2024 with Denali Edition 1. For 2026 GMC adds the lower-priced Elevation and the off-road AT4 trims, and introduces three battery options (Standard 120 kWh, Extended 170 kWh, Max 205 kWh).
models.model_summary: GMC's full-size electric pickup on GM's Ultium platform, riding 800V architecture with up to 350 kW DC fast charging. Three trims (Elevation, AT4, Denali) and three battery options (Standard 120 kWh, Extended 170 kWh, Max 205 kWh) deliver 283-478 miles EPA range and 605-760 hp. Innovative MultiPro Midgate extends bed length to nearly 11 feet.

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: brand-level (model too new for individual VDS)
reliability.summary: Sierra EV is too new for model-specific JD Power VDS (study measures 3-year-old vehicles). Brand-level: GMC ranked 24th overall at 228 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (industry avg 204). CR 2026 ranked GMC 23rd (31/100) — among the lowest-scoring brands. GM Ultium platform has had software/charging-system updates and recalls on the related Hummer EV in 2023-2024.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the Sierra EV's 350-kW DC fast charging (adds ~100 miles in 10 minutes), MultiPro Midgate feature, Super Cruise hands-free driving, and segment-leading 478-mile Max Range. The new lower-priced Elevation trim drops the entry MSRP below Ford F-150 Lightning, while the AT4 brings four-wheel steering, 35-inch tires, and a 2-inch lift for off-road duty. Knocks include hefty curb weight (~8,800 lb in Max Range) and price for top trims.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited owner reviews available as of research date; volume is low and most early customers received Denali Edition 1 builds.

notable_other: Energy Transfer Pro (10.2 kW off-board power)
Heated steering wheel and front seats
800V architecture with 350 kW DC fast charging

trims.notes: Lowest-MSRP Sierra EV configuration. 120 kWh Standard Range battery with 283 miles EPA range. Sole base trim of EV powertrain line. NHTSA/IIHS 2026 ratings not yet published; Sierra EV is a Crew Cab body style with frame derived from Silverado EV/Hummer EV BT1 platform — no specific crash testing available.

changes: 170 kWh Extended Range battery pack (vs 120 kWh Standard)

trims.notes: Extended Range battery pack option for Elevation. Shares image set with Elevation Standard Range. Towing capacity expands to 12,500 lb with the bigger battery.
Off-road AT4 Sierra EV trim. Comes with Extended Range battery and the higher-output 19.2 kW Level 2 AC charging.

notable_other: Nappa leather with massaging 16-way power front seats

changes: 170 kWh Extended Range battery (vs 120 kWh Standard)
Nappa leather with massaging 16-way power front seats
Heated/ventilated rear seats and power-down running boards

trims.notes: Mid-range Denali with Extended Range battery. EPA range 410 miles.

changes: 205 kWh Max Range battery (vs 120 kWh Standard)
760 hp / 785 lb-ft tri-motor performance setup (vs 605 hp / 765 lb-ft)
Shares Denali features (nappa leather, open-pore wood, Bose 14-speaker)

trims.notes: Flagship Sierra EV trim with 205 kWh Max Range battery for 478 miles EPA range. Shares Denali Extended Range trim-family images.

models.notes: Five trim configurations representing the five battery/trim combinations sold. Three trim_family groups (elevation, at4, denali) — Extended Range and Max Range battery variants of the same trim share image families. The Elevation Standard Range is the lowest MSRP and is therefore the EV powertrain line's sole base trim with is_base_trim:true. NHTSA/IIHS have not published 2026 ratings for Sierra EV; safety values null with notes. EPA fueleconomy.gov 2026 IDs all individually verified: 49660 (Standard Range), 49658 (Extended Range 11kW), 49659 (Extended Range 19kW or Max Range). All trims include Super Cruise standard, 800V architecture with 350 kW DC fast charging. Image entries marked needs_scraping for follow-up image extraction in Phase 4.

models.generation_context: Fifth generation, introduced 2021 with independent rear suspension. Mid-cycle refresh for 2025 brought a new exterior, larger 16.8-inch infotainment, and updated 3.0L Duramax diesel (305 hp). 2026 continues with the refreshed design and adds AT4 Ultimate trim.
models.model_summary: GMC's full-size three-row SUV, sharing the Chevrolet Tahoe / Cadillac Escalade T1XX platform. Three engine choices (5.3L V8, 6.2L V8, 3.0L Duramax I6 diesel) with a 10-speed automatic, and five trims spanning Elevation to Denali Ultimate. Standard Yukon offers 122.9 cu ft cargo behind the front row; XL version adds another 22.9 cu ft.

reliability.summary: GMC ranked 24th at 228 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (industry avg 204), a notable drop from 7th in 2025. CR 2026 ranked GMC 23rd (31/100). Consumer Reports predicts below-average reliability for the 2026 Yukon based on data from prior model years. Note: T1XX sibling Chevrolet Tahoe received a 2026 VDS large-SUV segment award, suggesting platform-level reliability is solid even if GMC brand average is poor.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: The 2026 Yukon is praised for spaciousness, smooth ride from independent rear suspension, and effective 10-speed automatic across all three engine choices. Edmunds rates it 6.7/10, citing strong towing (8,400 lb), comfortable interior, and the available Super Cruise hands-free system. Reviewers note that some owners report engine and infotainment issues, and value-per-dollar gets tighter at the Denali Ultimate's $103K starting price.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise spaciousness, comfortable ride, and the available Duramax diesel's fuel efficiency. Common complaints involve engine/electrical issues on early 5.3L V8 units and infotainment glitches; 63% of KBB-surveyed owners recommend the vehicle.

notable_other: Heated front seats and steering wheel

trims.notes: Base Yukon trim, 2WD standard. NHTSA awarded 4-star overall to 2026 Yukon. IIHS has not awarded TSP/TSP+ to a Yukon in recent years.

notable_other: Off-road suspension with adaptive ride control

trims.notes: Off-road Yukon trim. 4WD with 2-speed transfer case and adaptive ride control standard.
Luxury Yukon trim. 6.2L V8 standard; 3.0L Duramax diesel optional. Super Cruise hands-free is an option.

notable_other: AT4 unique exterior with all-terrain tires
Super Cruise standard with hands-free trailering
Nappa leather with massaging 16-way power front seats

trims.notes: New for 2026, combining AT4's off-road equipment with Ultimate-tier luxury features. 4WD only.

notable_other: Super Cruise standard with hands-free trailering
Full-grain Alpine Umber leather with massaging 16-way power front seats

trims.notes: Flagship Yukon. 6.2L V8 standard; 3.0L Duramax diesel optional. Super Cruise standard.

models.notes: Five trims, all on the standard Yukon wheelbase. NHTSA awarded 4-star overall to the 2026 Yukon. IIHS has not awarded TSP/TSP+. Three powertrain options share trim assignments — base 5.3L V8 in Elevation/AT4, 6.2L V8 standard in Denali/AT4 Ultimate/Denali Ultimate, 3.0L Duramax diesel optional across all trims. Image entries marked needs_scraping for follow-up image extraction in Phase 4.

models.generation_context: Fifth-generation extended-wheelbase Yukon, sharing all powertrains and tech with the standard Yukon but with a 130.0-inch wheelbase (vs 120.9 in.) and 225.2-inch overall length (vs 210.1 in.). 2026 is the second year of the refreshed look introduced in 2025.
models.model_summary: Extended-wheelbase variant of the Yukon, adding 15.1 inches of length, 9.1 inches of wheelbase, and 21.6 cu ft of cargo behind the third row. Same five trims (Elevation, AT4, Denali, AT4 Ultimate, Denali Ultimate) and same engine choices (5.3L V8, 6.2L V8, 3.0L Duramax diesel) as the standard Yukon.

reliability.summary: GMC ranked 24th at 228 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (industry avg 204). CR 2026 ranked GMC 23rd (31/100). Consumer Reports predicts below-average reliability for the 2026 Yukon XL based on data from prior years.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: The Yukon XL extends the standard Yukon's strengths into a much larger package with 144.5 cu ft of total cargo and easier third-row accommodation. Reviewers describe it as the practical choice for families who routinely carry 7+ passengers with luggage; reviewer scores mirror the standard Yukon at around 6.7/10 with the same praise for the refreshed interior and Super Cruise availability, and the same critiques about price and infotainment glitches.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited 2026 XL-specific owner reviews collected separately from standard Yukon. Owners highlight cargo capability and third-row comfort; complaints similar to standard Yukon.

notable_other: Heated front seats and steering wheel

trims.notes: Base Yukon XL trim. NHTSA awarded 4-star overall to the 2026 Yukon (XL shares basic structure).

notable_other: AT4 unique grille and all-terrain tires

trims.notes: Off-road XL trim. 4WD with 2-speed transfer case standard.
Luxury Yukon XL trim. 6.2L V8 standard; 3.0L Duramax diesel optional. Super Cruise hands-free is an option.

notable_other: AT4 unique exterior with all-terrain tires
Super Cruise standard with hands-free trailering
Nappa leather with massaging 16-way power front seats

trims.notes: Combines AT4's off-road equipment with Ultimate-tier luxury. 4WD only.

notable_other: Super Cruise standard with hands-free trailering
Full-grain Alpine Umber leather with massaging 16-way power front seats

trims.notes: Flagship Yukon XL. 6.2L V8 standard; 3.0L Duramax diesel optional.

models.notes: Same five trims as Yukon, sharing all powertrains and ADAS, with stretched 130.0-inch wheelbase and 225.2-inch overall length. Cargo capacity grows from 122.9 cu ft to 144.5 cu ft total. Separate EPA fueleconomy.gov 2026 IDs verified for XL: 49880 (6.2L 2WD), 49910 (6.2L 4WD), 49882 (5.3L 2WD), 49912 (5.3L 4WD), 49883 (Duramax diesel 2WD), 49913 (Duramax diesel 4WD). Image entries marked needs_scraping for follow-up image extraction in Phase 4.

models.generation_context: Third generation, all-new for 2025 model year on GM's VSS-A platform shared with the Chevrolet Equinox. 2026 introduces AT4 (off-road) and Denali trims, expanding from a single Elevation trim in 2025. Single 1.5L turbo I4 powertrain across the lineup.
models.model_summary: GMC's compact crossover SUV, all-new for 2025 with VSS-A platform shared with Chevy Equinox. One 1.5L turbo I4 (175 hp / 184 lb-ft FWD or 203 lb-ft AWD), three trims (Elevation, AT4, Denali), CVT on FWD and 8-speed automatic on AWD. New AT4 and Denali trims for 2026 add off-road and luxury options to the lineup.

reliability.summary: GMC ranked 24th at 228 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (industry avg 204). CR 2026 ranked GMC 23rd (31/100), among the lowest-scoring brands. Consumer Reports stripped its Recommended rating from the 2026 Terrain after dismal reliability data from owners (CR flagged GMC Terrain/Acadia as 'well-below-average reliability' in the 2026 Brand Report Card).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: The all-new Terrain has a more upscale interior and tech (15-inch touchscreen, 11-inch driver display, wireless CarPlay/Android Auto) than the prior generation, with a single turbo I4 engine simplifying the lineup. Reviewers note that the 175-hp output is modest for a 3,500-lb crossover, and Consumer Reports flagged reliability concerns severe enough to strip the Recommended rating. The new AT4 (off-road) and Denali (luxury) trims expand appeal beyond the 2025-only Elevation.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited owner reviews to date. Owners praise interior space, ride comfort, and the large infotainment screen; some complain about modest acceleration and complex menu structures.

notable_other: GMC Pro Safety standard (AEB, lane keep, lane departure)

trims.notes: Base Terrain trim. FWD standard; AWD optional for $2,000 more (8-speed automatic vs CVT). NHTSA/IIHS have not yet published 2026 Terrain ratings.

notable_other: Leather seating with heated steering wheel

trims.notes: New off-road trim for 2026. AWD with 8-speed automatic standard.
New luxury trim for 2026. AWD with 8-speed automatic standard.

models.notes: Three trims, all with the same 1.5L turbo I4 engine. CVT on FWD Elevation, 8-speed automatic on AWD Elevation, AT4, and Denali. Consumer Reports stripped its Recommended rating in late 2025 due to dismal reliability data. NHTSA/IIHS have not published 2026 ratings; 2025 Terrain is also a new third-gen vehicle so prior-MY ratings do not transfer. EPA fueleconomy.gov 2026 IDs all verified: 49446 (FWD), 49485 (AWD Elevation 8-speed), 49486 (AWD AT4/Denali). Image entries marked needs_scraping for follow-up image extraction in Phase 4.

models.generation_context: Third generation, introduced for 2024 model year on the GM C1XX platform with a significantly larger footprint (10.6 inches longer than 2023). Single 2.5L turbo I4 powertrain (328 hp / 326 lb-ft) with 8-speed automatic. 2026 carries over with price increases and minor refinements.
models.model_summary: GMC's three-row midsize SUV, fully redesigned for 2024 with a larger 204-inch length on a 120.9-inch wheelbase. Single 2.5L turbo I4 (328 hp / 326 lb-ft) with 8-speed automatic, FWD standard / AWD optional (or standard on AT4). Four trims (Elevation, AT4, Denali, Denali Ultimate). Up to 97.5 cu ft cargo with seats folded.

reliability.summary: GMC ranked 24th at 228 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (industry avg 204). CR 2026 ranked GMC 23rd (31/100); CR specifically flagged GMC Acadia and Terrain as having 'well-below-average reliability' in the 2026 Brand Report Card. Consumer Reports stripped Recommended rating from the 2026 Acadia citing recurring transmission, brakes, electrical accessory, and in-car electronics issues.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the redesigned Acadia's roomier cabin (especially third row), upscale 15-inch infotainment, and standard 328-hp turbo I4 power. Edmunds rates it 6.7/10, noting comfortable seating across all rows and reasonable cargo. Notable concerns: Consumer Reports stripped its Recommended status due to transmission and electrical reliability issues, and IIHS denied 2026 Top Safety Pick (which 2025 Acadia earned) due to tightened rear-seat criteria.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the comfortable ride, spacious interior, and modern tech; common complaints involve infotainment glitches and transmission roughness. 50% of KBB-surveyed owners recommend the vehicle.

notable_other: Heated front seats and steering wheel

trims.notes: Base Acadia trim. FWD standard; AWD optional. NHTSA 5-star overall; IIHS withheld 2026 TSP after tightening moderate overlap rear-seat criteria (2025 Acadia had earned TSP). 8-passenger seating with 2nd-row bench.

notable_other: Standard AWD with twin-clutch rear drive

trims.notes: Off-road Acadia trim. AWD standard.
Luxury Acadia trim. FWD standard; AWD optional. Super Cruise hands-free is an option.

notable_other: Super Cruise standard with hands-free trailering
Full-grain nappa leather with massaging 16-way power front seats

trims.notes: Flagship Acadia trim. AWD standard with Super Cruise hands-free included.

models.notes: Four trims, all with the single 2.5L turbo I4 engine and 8-speed automatic. NHTSA awarded 5-star overall to the 2026 Acadia. IIHS denied 2026 Top Safety Pick due to tightened moderate overlap rear-seat criteria — the 2025 Acadia had earned TSP under prior rules. Consumer Reports stripped Recommended rating in late 2025 due to reliability issues. EPA fueleconomy.gov 2026 IDs verified: 49552 (FWD), 49580 (AWD). Image entries marked needs_scraping for follow-up image extraction in Phase 4.

models.generation_context: Resurrected Hummer nameplate as an electric vehicle on GM's Ultium platform. Production began 2021 with limited Edition 1 trim, expanded to 2X/3X for 2024+. 2026 features the Carbon Fiber Edition option package on 3X. Built at GM's Factory ZERO in Detroit-Hamtramck.
models.model_summary: GMC's halo electric pickup, a 4-door 5-passenger crew cab with two trims (2X, 3X) and an available Carbon Fiber Edition package on 3X. Up to 1,160 hp tri-motor setup and 'Watts to Freedom' launch mode (0-60 in ~3 seconds). 800V Ultium architecture supports 350 kW DC fast charging. Optional CrabWalk four-wheel-steering, Extract Mode air suspension to lift 6 inches, and infinity roof targa panels.

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: brand-level (model too low-volume for individual VDS)
reliability.summary: Hummer EV is too low-volume for individual JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports reliability sampling. Brand-level: GMC ranked 24th overall at 228 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (industry avg 204). CR 2026 ranked GMC 23rd (31/100) among the lowest-scoring brands. The Hummer EV had multiple battery/software-related recalls in 2023-2024.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July); Hummer EV is also too low-volume for individual APEAL coverage.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Hummer EV Pickup's spectacle: tri-motor 1,000+ hp performance, CrabWalk four-wheel steering, Extract Mode air suspension, and removable infinity roof panels. Critiques focus on the 9,000+ lb curb weight that produces poor 50-53 MPGe efficiency, the truck's enormous footprint that makes everyday use cumbersome, and the $100K+ pricing for a vehicle still limited to a 5-foot bed.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited owner-review data due to low volume; early owners are enthusiasts willing to overlook efficiency and size.

notable_other: Air suspension with adjustable ride height

trims.notes: Base Pickup trim, dual-motor AWD with 570 hp. fueleconomy.gov has not yet published a specific 2026 entry for this trim as of 2026-05-13; MPGe values use the 2025 EPA test data (id=48343) since the 2026 model is mechanically identical and source set to the EPA brand model-browse page until EPA publishes.

notable_other: Carbon Fiber Edition option package available

trims.notes: Top Hummer EV Pickup trim. Carbon Fiber Edition is a $13,700 option package (not a separate trim per spec rules) that increases output to 1,160 hp / 13,000 lb-ft and adds Carbon Fiber styling. fueleconomy.gov has not yet published a specific 2026 entry; MPGe values use the 2025 EPA test data (id=48344) since the 2026 model is mechanically identical.

models.notes: Two trims (2X and 3X), with Carbon Fiber Edition as a $13,700 option package on 3X (not a separate trim per spec rules). NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested any Hummer EV Pickup or SUV due to low volume and weight class (over 10,000 lb GVWR). fueleconomy.gov has not yet published 2026 entries for Hummer EV; MPGe and range values use 2025 EPA test data (ids 48343 and 48344) per the EPA-unavailable fallback rule, since the 2026 model is mechanically identical to 2025. Image entries marked needs_scraping for follow-up image extraction in Phase 4.

models.generation_context: Resurrected Hummer nameplate as an SUV variant of the Hummer EV Pickup. Production began for 2024 MY at GM's Factory ZERO. Shares Ultium platform, electric motors, and 800V architecture with the Pickup but on a 9-inch shorter wheelbase and with a closed cargo area.
models.model_summary: GMC's halo electric SUV, a 4-door 5-passenger crossover-style vehicle riding the same Ultium platform as the Hummer EV Pickup. Two trims (2X, 3X) with optional Extreme Off-Road and Carbon Fiber Edition packages. Tri-motor 3X delivers up to 830 hp; air suspension with Extract Mode lifts to 16-inch ground clearance. Removable Infinity Roof targa panels.

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: brand-level (model too low-volume for individual VDS)
reliability.summary: Hummer EV SUV is too low-volume for individual JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports reliability sampling. Brand-level: GMC ranked 24th overall at 228 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (industry avg 204). CR 2026 ranked GMC 23rd (31/100) among the lowest-scoring brands. The Hummer EV nameplate had multiple battery/software-related recalls in 2023-2024.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July); Hummer EV SUV is also too low-volume for individual APEAL coverage.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Hummer EV SUV as a more usable everyday version of the Pickup — same outrageous power and off-road tech (CrabWalk, Extract Mode, removable Infinity Roof) but with a closed cargo area and slightly more livable proportions. Critiques mirror the Pickup: 9,000+ lb curb weight, modest 52-53 MPGe efficiency, and a $100K+ price for a vehicle still impractical in city use.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited owner-review data due to low production volume.

notable_other: Air suspension with adjustable ride height

trims.notes: Base SUV trim, dual-motor AWD with 570 hp. Extreme Off-Road package optional ($+) and increases output to 635 hp / 9,900 lb-ft. fueleconomy.gov has not yet published a specific 2026 entry; MPGe values use 2025 EPA test data (id=48347) per EPA-unavailable fallback rule.

notable_other: Tri-motor system standard (830 hp / 11,500 lb-ft at wheels)
Extract Mode air suspension (lifts to 16-inch ground clearance)
Carbon Fiber Edition option package available

trims.notes: Top Hummer EV SUV trim. Carbon Fiber Edition option package available. fueleconomy.gov has not yet published a specific 2026 entry; MPGe values use 2025 EPA test data (id=48348) per EPA-unavailable fallback rule.

models.notes: Two trims (2X and 3X), with Carbon Fiber Edition option package on 3X (not a separate trim per spec rules). NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested any Hummer EV due to low volume and weight class (over 10,000 lb GVWR). fueleconomy.gov 2026 entries not yet published; MPGe and range values use 2025 EPA test data (ids 48347 and 48348) per EPA-unavailable fallback rule, since the 2026 model is mechanically identical to 2025. The Hummer EV SUV body style is treated as a separate model from the Pickup per the body-style rule. Image entries marked needs_scraping for follow-up image extraction in Phase 4.
